before_code:
have hdet : (g.det.val : ℝ) = g.val.det := …
rw [show (k + 2 - 1 : ℤ) = (k - 1) + 2 by ring, …]
after_code:
have hdet : g.det.val = g.val.det := …
rw [show k + 2 - 1 = (k - 1) + 2 by ring, …]
description: After writing or cleaning a proof, make sure there are no unnecessary type casts that can be removed. Audit every explicit `(e : T)` ascription and `↑` coercion: an ascription that merely restates the type `e` already has is redundant and should be dropped — e.g. `(g.det.val : ℝ)` when `g.det.val` is already ℝ (it is `.val` of an `ℝˣ`), or `(k + 2 - 1 : ℤ)` / `(-(k + 2) : ℤ)` when `k : ℤ` already forces ℤ. Keep only genuine cross-type coercions that bridge different types (ℝ→ℂ, ℤ→ℂ, ℍ→ℂ, …) and ascriptions that are load-bearing for elaboration (e.g. a `: ℂ → ℂ` codomain annotation that fixes the target of a `↑`).
